system.description:
Activate your Virtue when you meet a character (even a Villain) for the first time. She treats you as friendly for one scene.

Arcana: The Road.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero decides he cannot take an action without help from another Hero—insisting another Hero spend a Hero Point to give you Bonus Dice, or asking her to use one of her Advantages to aid you, for example.

Arcana: The Road.

Activate your Virtue and target a Villain. Until the end of this Scene, you cannot spend Hero Points and the Villain cannot spend Danger Points.

Arcana: The Magician.

You receive a Hero Point when you chase after power and the deal you're after is dangerous or causes trouble.

Arcana: The Magician.

Activate your Virtue when another Hero takes Wounds to prevent her from suffering those Wounds. You take one Dramatic Wound instead.

Arcana: The Lovers.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero becomes enamored with someone she really shouldn't.

Arcana: The Lovers.

Activate your Virtue to escape danger from the current Scene. You cannot rescue anyone but yourself.

Arcana: The Fool.

You receive a Hero Point when you investigate something unusual, especially if it looks dangerous.

Arcana: The Fool.

Activate your Virtue to delay an Opportunity or a Consequence by 1 Action.

Arcana: The Wheel.

You receive 2 Hero Points when you choose to fail an important Risk before rolling.

Arcana: The Wheel.

Activate your Virtue after a Villain spends Raises for an Action. That Action fails. The Villain still loses the Raises she spent.

Arcana: The Devil.

You receive a Hero Point when you accept someone's lies or lopsided deal.

Arcana: The Devil.

Activate your Virtue to gain 2 Hero Points instead of 1 when you activate your Hubris or trigger a Quirk.

Arcana: The Tower.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero shows disdain, contempt, or otherwise looks down.

Arcana: The Tower.

Activate your Virtue to discover a Brute Squad's type, or to know a Villain's Rank and Advantages.

Arcana: The Beggar.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero covets something, and does something unwise to get it.

Arcana: The Beggar.

Activate your Virtue to ask the GM one yes or no question about an NPC. The GM must answer honestly and should be generous—for example, if there is a qualifier, he should tell you and explain more fully.

Arcana: The Witch.

You receive a Hero Point when you try to get someone else to do your dirty work for you, and it backfires.

Arcana: The Witch.

Activate your Virtue the first time you Wound a Villain during a fight to make her take a Dramatic Wound in addition to the Wounds you normally deal.

Arcana: The War.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero goes back for a fallen comrade or refuses to leave a wounded ally.

Arcana: The War.

Activate your Virtue to suffer a Risk's Consequences in place of another Hero.

Arcana: The Hanged Man.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero takes an Action to pause in hesitation, doubt, or uncertainty before she makes a move.

Arcana: The Hanged Man.

Activate your Virtue to take your first Action before anyone else in a Round.

Arcana: Coins (for the Ferryman).

You receive a Hero Point when you refuse to leave well enough alone or quit while you're ahead, and it gets you into trouble.

Arcana: Coins (for the Ferryman).

Activate your Virtue to cancel the effects of Fear on you and your friends.

Arcana: The Thrones.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero is stubborn and refuses to change her mind in the face of evidence.

Arcana: The Thrones.

Activate your Virtue when you act behind the scenes, from the shadows, or through a proxy. For the next Risk, when you determine Raises, every die counts as a Raise.

Arcana: The Moonless Night.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero fails to understand an important plot element and that misunderstanding leads to danger for herself or others.

Arcana: The Moonless Night.

Activate your Virtue when you are the center of attention. For the next Risk, when you determine Raises, every die counts as a Raise.

Arcana: The Sun.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero refuses an offer of aid—for example, if a Hero tries to spend a Hero Point to give you Bonus Dice and you turn them down.

Arcana: The Sun.

Activate your Virtue to know whenever any other character lies to you until the end of the Scene.

Arcana: The Prophet.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero strongly defends one of her opinions when the time or place is inappropriate.

Arcana: The Prophet.

Activate your Virtue and choose another Hero in the same scene to pool your Raises for the round, spending Raises to take Actions from your shared pool.

Arcana: Reunion.

You receive a Hero Point when you bring up old grudges or bad feelings when doing so will lead to trouble.

Arcana: Reunion.

Activate your Virtue to add Bonus Dice to your Risk equal to the Fear rating of your target.

Arcana: The Hero.

You receive a Hero Point when your brash, cocky, or reckless actions cause trouble for you and another Hero.

Arcana: The Hero.

Activate your Virtue to prevent any magical effect (Sorcery, Artifacts, Monsters, etc.) from affecting you.

Arcana: The Glyph.

You receive a Hero Point when you refuse to solve a problem using Sorcery, an artifact, or some other mystical effect that you don't trust.

Arcana: The Glyph.

Activate your Virtue. The GM gives a Hero Point to all other Heroes in this Scene.

Arcana: The Emperor.

You receive a Hero Point when your Hero flies off the handle and loses her temper, causing trouble.

Arcana: The Emperor.
